tabs.label: Insurance
tabs.headline: Policies, renewals, and claims in one pipeline.
tabs.body: Track carriers and products alongside every policy, with renewal reminders before they lapse.
tabs.quote: We stopped missing renewal windows because the reminder lives next to the policy, not in a spreadsheet.

tabs.label: Real Estate
tabs.headline: Match clients to properties, not just contacts.
tabs.body: Viewings, offers, and contracts stay attached to both the client and the property.
tabs.quote: Every offer has the full viewing history attached — no more piecing it together from email.
tabs.author: Fictional demo persona

tabs.label: Immigration
tabs.headline: Cases, deadlines, and required documents tracked together.
tabs.body: Each case carries its visa type, authority submissions, and document checklist in one place.
tabs.quote: Deadlines used to live in someone's calendar. Now they live on the case itself.

tabs.label: Barber Shop

tabs.label: Beauty Salon

tabs.body: Before/after photos and signed consent forms travel with the client record.

tabs.label: Restaurant
tabs.headline: Reservations, allergies, and catering leads together.

tabs.label: Sales & Agency

tabs.body: Proposals, performance, and personalized campaigns share the same contact and deal data.

faq.headline: What teams ask before they start.

items.q: Do I need an AI API key?
items.a: No. Every AI feature has a graceful, keyless fallback — lead scoring, content suggestions, and drafts still work; connecting a real provider just improves quality.

items.q: Is my data isolated from other tenants?
items.a: Yes. Tenant isolation is enforced at the data layer, not just per-screen — it's structurally impossible for one workspace to read another's records.

items.q: Can I activate only the modules I need?
items.a: Yes. Insurance, Real Estate, Immigration, and the service-booking modules are each opt-in per workspace.
The workspace starts with realistic fictional data. You can test selected actions in your browser, but nothing is saved to the database or sent to external services.

items.q: Is the contract template legally reviewed?
items.a: It's a generated starting point, not legal advice — review any contract with your own counsel before sending it to a real client.

cta.subhead: Explore a complete demo workspace with leads, deals, contracts, and campaigns already connected.

footer.tagline: Every lead, carried to a close.

finance.title: Finance
finance.description: Revenue rollups across your pipeline and contracts, plus billing status.

industries.title: Industry templates
industries.description: Apply a ready-made industry solution — it creates configured entities and sample data you can customize. Built on the low-code kernel, not hardcoded.

leads.title: About Leads
leads.body:
A lead is a raw, unqualified inquiry — it arrives automatically from your public website form, or you can add one manually. Leads are never edited to look presentable; they stay exactly as received so you always see the original context.

From this page you can:
- Open a lead to see its full details: contact info, submission date, field of work, and current status.
- Score it with AI — get a 0-100 fit score plus a short rationale based on how complete the information is and what stage of business it represents.
- Get an AI content suggestion — a relevant article and a ready-to-send follow-up message.
- Convert it into a Contact (and, if there's a business name, a Company) - optionally creating a Deal at the same time.

Why this matters: converting is a deliberate, one-way step. The lead itself is never deleted or overwritten, so you always have a clean trail from first contact to customer.

contacts.title: About Contacts
contacts.body:
A contact is a real person you're in a relationship with — a customer, prospect, or anyone converted from a lead. This is the 360-degree view: everything about them in one place.

From this page you can:
- See a full timeline from the moment they first arrived as a lead through conversion and every activity since — calls, notes, tasks, and stage changes, oldest to newest.
- Add an activity or a task by hand, e.g. a reminder for what to do next with this person.
- Read their AI "customer knowledge" summary, generated from a linked chatbot conversation.
- See which company they belong to (if any) and jump straight to it.

The list view shows where each contact came from (website, manual entry, chatbot, Telegram, web chat) and their lifecycle stage, so you can tell a brand-new prospect from an existing customer at a glance.

companies.title: About Companies
companies.body:
A company is the organization a contact belongs to — created automatically when a lead with a business name is converted, or added directly here.

From this page you can:
- See every contact who works there, and every deal tied to it.
- Add a call, meeting, or task the same way you would on a contact — it appears on the company's own timeline.
- See a rolled-up timeline that also includes activity logged on any of its contacts, labeled with that contact's name, so nothing about the relationship gets lost across individual people.

deals.title: About the Deals pipeline
deals.body:
Every deal moves through 5 active stages, left to right: New, Reviewing, Consultation, Proposal sent, and Final contract phase. Once a deal is decided, it leaves the board entirely and becomes either Won or Lost.

You can move a deal to a different stage two ways:
- Drag and drop the card onto another column.
- Tap its "Options" button and pick a stage directly — the more reliable way on a touchscreen, and it can also jump straight to Won or Lost.

Below the board, the closed & pending summary totals every deal that's currently won, lost, or still open, with the amount attached to each.

activityFeed.title: About the Activity feed
activityFeed.body:
The Activity feed is a single chronological stream of everything meaningful happening across the workspace — new and converted leads, contacts and companies created or updated, deals moving through the pipeline, tasks, files, appointments, contracts, campaigns, and manually logged calls, meetings, emails, and notes.

Each entry shows who did it and when, and — where relevant — links straight through to the record it happened on.

From this page you can:
- Filter by category (contacts, deals, tasks, logged activities, and more) and by date range.
- Log a new activity yourself — pick the contact, company, deal, or lead it relates to, choose a type (call, meeting, email, or note), and it's recorded on that record's own timeline as well as here.

Why this matters: it's the fastest way to see what's actually happening across the business without opening every record individually.

contracts.title: About Contracts
contracts.body:
Every contract starts from a template — Consulting, Software development, Monthly retainer, or One-time project — and can be edited further as Markdown before it's finalized.

Before a contract can be shared, it must be digitally signed: configure your company's letterhead (logo, address) and signatory once from "Letterhead & signature", then apply the signature to each contract. Only after that can you send it — a share link is never issued for an unsigned contract.

Once sent, the client reviews the contract on a public page carrying your letterhead and, at the end, the signature block with a QR code. They accept by entering their own name and the contract number shown on the page.

From this page you can also:
- Download a watermarked pre-contract PDF for informal client review — it never carries the digital signature.
- Download the final signed PDF once the contract has been signed.
- Get an AI-drafted follow-up message for a contract that's been sent but not yet accepted.

campaigns.title: About Email campaigns
campaigns.body:
A campaign targets a segment — a live, code-driven audience like lost leads, leads unfollowed for over a week, lost deals, or won customers — and AI writes a personalized email for every recipient, one at a time, based on their own situation in the CRM.

Before creating a campaign, the segment picker shows the true size of that audience and how many are reachable by email; each run is capped at 20 recipients.

From the review page you can:
- Generate a draft for one recipient, or generate all pending drafts at once (still one request at a time, so no burst hits the AI provider).
- Edit any draft before it goes out.
- Send one approved draft, or send every ready draft in one action, once you've reviewed them.

Why this matters: nothing is ever sent without a human having generated (and optionally edited) that specific message first — even "send all" only sends drafts that were already generated and approved.

reports.title: About Reports
reports.body:
Tabular reports (deals, pipeline, tasks, contacts) give you the raw rows behind each number, exportable as CSV. "Visual insights" turns the same underlying data into charts: a conversion funnel from lead through won deal, pipeline value by stage, where your leads come from, and monthly revenue over the last 12 months.

Why this matters: the tables are for auditing exact figures or exporting to another tool; the charts are for spotting a trend or a bottleneck at a glance.

knowledge.title: Knowledge base
knowledge.body:
The knowledge base is a small library of your own reference articles — product notes, FAQs, how-tos.

The AI content-suggestion skill reads these when drafting a follow-up to a lead: it scores every article for relevance, picks the best match, and grounds its message in that article's real content instead of inventing facts.

- Keep articles short and focused on one topic
- Add tags so the matcher can find them
- Nothing here is shown publicly — it only feeds internal AI suggestions

graph.title: Relationships
graph.body:
This is a visual map of how your records connect — which contacts work at which company, and which deals belong to them.

Each dot is a record; each line is a relationship. It's useful for seeing the whole picture around an account at a glance: everyone involved and every deal in play, without clicking through pages.

- Companies, contacts, and deals are colour-coded
- The map fills in automatically as you link records
- If you rarely work with multi-person accounts, you may not need this view

operations.title: Operations
operations.body:
The Operations workspace is where the day-to-day work lives — the things that aren't a specific customer record.

- Tasks: your to-dos, with due dates, priorities, subtasks, and recurrence
- Calendar: appointments and deadlines, with booking-conflict detection
- Files: secure document storage with categories and expiry

This overview rolls them up so you can see what's open, what's overdue, and what's coming this week.

studio.title: Studio
studio.body:
Studio is the low-code home of the platform — where you shape the system to your business without engineering.

- Industry templates: apply a ready-made vertical (e.g. dental clinic) that sets up entities and sample data
- Entities: create your own record types with custom fields
- Forms, Workflows, Automations, Rules, Templates: build the logic behind them

Changes here are versioned, so you can review history and roll back.

finance.title: Finance
finance.body:
The Finance workspace rolls up the money side of the CRM across every module.

- Won revenue and open pipeline value from your deals
- Total contract value, broken down by status
- Win rate and recently won deals
- Your workspace's billing and subscription status

It's a read-only summary — the numbers come from the deals, contracts, and billing you manage elsewhere.

workflows.title: Workflow builder
workflows.body:
A workflow is a staged process — intake, review, approval, and so on — that a loan, policy, case, or deal moves through.

Each stage can require specific documents before it can advance, route to an approver role, and carry an SLA (a target number of hours before it's considered overdue).

- Drag stages into the order that matches your real process
- Required documents are enforced before a record can move to the next stage
- Publishing makes the workflow live immediately for new and existing records of that type

contractsDetail.title: Contract detail
contractsDetail.body:
This is a single contract's full record — its content, status, signature, and sharing history.

- Edit the body while it is still a draft; once accepted, editing locks
- The status stepper shows where it stands: draft, signed, sent, viewed, accepted
- Use the print/export actions to get a pre-contract preview or the final signed PDF

rules.title: Business rules
rules.body:
A business rule is a reusable check — validation, eligibility, approval, or a calculation — expressed once and evaluated everywhere it applies: in forms, workflows, and automations.

- Rules use a safe expression syntax, not arbitrary code
- The same rule can gate a form field, a workflow stage, and an automation condition
- Changes are versioned so you can review history and roll back

templates.title: Templates
templates.body:
Templates are reusable content — emails, documents, notifications, reports, and AI prompts — with `{{variable}}` placeholders filled in from the record they're used on.

- Live variable detection shows which placeholders a template actually uses
- The same template engine powers contracts, campaign emails, and AI prompts
- Editing a template does not change documents already generated from it

industries.title: Industry templates
industries.body:
An industry template applies a ready-made vertical solution — configured entities, fields, and sample data — built on the same low-code kernel as the Entity Builder.

- Applying a template creates real entities and demo records you can edit or delete
- It is a starting point, not a locked-in module — everything it creates is yours to customize
- Safe to explore: nothing here affects your existing contacts, deals, or other data

imports.title: Contact imports
imports.body:
Upload a CSV file to bulk-create or update contacts instead of entering them one at a time.

- Map your file's columns to CRM fields before importing
- Each row is validated; the import report shows what succeeded and what was skipped, with a reason
- Large files are processed in the background — you can leave the page and check back

platformAdmin.title: Platform administration
platformAdmin.body:
This cross-tenant panel is visible only to the platform super admin — it shapes what every visitor and tenant sees on the public site and pricing page, not just your own workspace.

- Pricing plans, landing page copy, branding, and the announcement bar are all editable here without a code deploy
- An empty field always falls back to the built-in default text
- Changes take effect immediately for all visitors

ai.title: AI Operating System
ai.body:
Skills draft — a human always approves before anything changes.

- The Action Center holds every AI-proposed write until you approve or reject it
- Without an API key configured, a keyless mock provider still produces useful heuristic output, so the feature is always demonstrable
- Every AI call is logged for audit, including which skill ran and what it produced

aiAssistant.title: CRM Assistant
aiAssistant.body:
A tool-calling chat assistant that answers questions using your real CRM data — leads, deals, and activity — never invented numbers.

- The assistant calls read-only tools to fetch real figures before answering; it can't fabricate a statistic
- It has no write access — it can't create, edit, or delete anything
- Try one of the suggested questions to see it in action

settings.title: Settings
settings.body:
Your workspace's company profile and brand identity.

- The workspace name appears throughout the app and on shared documents
- Brand color and corner radius customize the look for everyone in this tenant, not just you
- Changes here don't affect other tenants — each workspace has its own identity

audit.title: Audit log
audit.body:
An immutable, security-relevant trail of who did what — sign-ins, permission changes, data exports, and admin actions.

- Every entry is categorized (auth, data, file, permission, export, admin, security, AI) for quick filtering by eye
- This log can't be edited or deleted from the app — it's the compliance record
- Useful for investigating an incident or answering "who changed this?"

health.title: System health
health.body:
Live operational signals for this workspace — background jobs, automation runs, AI usage, and webhook delivery.

- A failed job or automation run doesn't silently disappear — it shows up here
- Failing webhooks are flagged so you know an integration needs attention
- Recent security & permission events give an early warning if something needs a closer look

integrations.title: Integrations
integrations.body:
Connect SOBI CRM to other tools — send data out via webhooks, or let other systems read your data via the public API.

- A webhook fires an HTTP request to your endpoint whenever a chosen event happens (e.g. a deal is won)
- Deliveries are signed and retried automatically if your endpoint is briefly unavailable
- An API key is shown once at creation — store it somewhere safe, since it can't be revealed again

modules.title: Modules
modules.body:
Activate the industry modules your business actually needs — each one adds its own workspace, pipeline, and dashboard.

- Activating a module doesn't affect other tenants or cost anything extra in this demo
- Modules marked "coming soon" are on the roadmap but not buildable yet
- Deactivating a module hides its workspace but keeps its data intact

roles.title: Roles & permissions
roles.body:
A role is a named bundle of permissions assigned to members — it decides what someone can see and do.

- System roles (Owner, Administrator, Manager, Employee, Client) are always available and can't be deleted
- A member can hold more than one role; their access is the union of all of them
- Permission keys follow a "<module>.<entity>.<action>" pattern, with wildcard support

users.title: Users & teams
users.body:
Everyone with access to this workspace, their type, and the roles that control what they can do.

- Staff are internal team members; External and Client are for portal/partner-style access
- A suspended member keeps their account but loses access until reactivated
- Role assignment happens per member — check Roles & Permissions to see what each role actually grants

entities.title: Entity builder
entities.body:
Create your own record type — with its own fields, list view, detail records, and a generated API — without writing code.

- A new entity immediately gets a working list view and create form based on its field definitions
- Field types (text, number, currency, date, select, and more) drive validation automatically
- Every custom entity gets the same timeline, search, and API support as built-in ones like Contacts

automations.title: Automations
automations.body:
An automation is a simple recipe: when something happens, automatically do something else — no manual follow-through required.

- Choose a trigger event (e.g. "Deal won") and an action (e.g. "Create a task")
- Every run is logged, so you can see exactly what fired and when
- Turn an automation off anytime without losing its configuration

dashboard.title: CRM dashboard
dashboard.body:
Your workspace's home page — a live snapshot of what's happening across leads, deals, and activity right now.

- KPI cards, the pipeline chart, and activity trend update automatically as records change
- "Customize" lets you rearrange, add, or remove widgets — your layout is personal, not shared
- The needs-attention panel surfaces things a manager should probably look at (overdue tasks, stalled deals, unassigned leads)

forms.title: Form builder
forms.body:
Design the fields and layout of any entity's create/edit form — no code required.

- Add fields, arrange them into sections, and set conditional visibility or validation
- Changes are versioned, so you can review history and roll back a form to an earlier version
- Publishing a form applies it immediately to that entity everywhere it's used

entitiesRecords.title: Custom entity records
entitiesRecords.body:
Every record created for a custom entity you built in Studio shows up here as a plain list.

- Columns come straight from the entity's own field definitions
- Use the button in the header to add a new record
- Go back to Studio to add fields, change the layout, or manage permissions

dashboardCustomize.title: Customize dashboard
dashboardCustomize.body:
Rearrange, resize, or remove the widgets on your management dashboard.

- Drag a widget by its header to move it; drag a corner to resize
- Changes save automatically and are private to your role unless shared
- Use "Back to dashboard" to return to the live view at any time

billing.title: Billing & plans
billing.body:
See your workspace's current plan and compare it with what else is available.

- The highlighted plan is the one your workspace is on right now
- Upgrading unlocks the features listed on each plan card
- Billing changes are logged for your workspace owner to review

profile.title: Your profile
profile.body:
Your personal account details and the workspaces you belong to.

- Name and email come from your account, not from any single workspace
- "Workspaces" lists every tenant your account has a membership in
- Plan details here mirror the Billing page for the active workspace

support.title: Support center
support.body:
Reach the Sobinta team for help, and keep every past conversation in one place.

- Start a new ticket, or use live chat if your plan includes it
- Replies arrive in the same thread, with unread requests marked
- In the demo workspace, sending a message only simulates a reply locally

moduleGeneric.title: Industry module
moduleGeneric.body:
This module is active for your workspace but doesn't have a bespoke dashboard yet — it still works, composed from the same shared engines (entities, workflows, and rules) as every other module.

- Your data is real and already being tracked, even on this generic view
- Bespoke screens land over time, following the same patterns as Insurance and the CRM
- Studio lets you customize its entities and fields today, before a dedicated dashboard exists

moduleBarber.title: Barber Shop
moduleBarber.body:
Run a barbershop's day-to-day from one place: book appointments, keep the chair schedule moving, and manage the services you offer.

- Appointments track the customer, chosen service, and time slot
- Services define what's bookable, with duration and price
- The dashboard surfaces what's upcoming today and what's been completed

moduleSalon.title: Beauty Salon
moduleSalon.body:
Manage a beauty salon's treatments and client appointments without leaving the CRM.

- Treatments define what clients can book, with duration and price
- Appointments link a client to a treatment and a time slot
- The dashboard shows what's upcoming, what's happening today, and what's done

moduleRestaurant.title: Restaurant
moduleRestaurant.body:
Track table reservations and seating for a restaurant.

- A reservation records the guest's name, party size, and time
- The dashboard shows upcoming reservations alongside today's and completed covers
- Built on the same booking engine as Barber Shop and Beauty Salon, tuned for parties instead of services

moduleInsurance.title: Insurance
moduleInsurance.body:
Manage insurance policies from quote to renewal.

- A policy tracks its product line, premium, commission, and expiry date
- Renewals due within 30 days surface automatically so nothing lapses unnoticed
- The dashboard rolls up active policies, annual commission, and open claims

moduleLoans.title: Loan & Banking
moduleLoans.body:
Track loan applications from intake through disbursement, and the bank partners they're routed to.

- An application records the applicant, purpose, amount, and requested term
- Status moves from draft through review to approved, rejected, or disbursed
- The dashboard shows open applications, financed volume, and what's pending review

moduleRealestate.title: Real Estate
moduleRealestate.body:
Manage a property portfolio: listings, viewings, and sale status.

- A property records its type, price, bedrooms, and address
- Viewings link a visitor to a property and a scheduled time
- The dashboard tracks what's available, reserved, sold, and total listed value

moduleImmigration.title: Immigration
moduleImmigration.body:
Track visa and permit cases from intake to decision.

- A case records the client, visa type, issuing authority, and deadline
- Approaching deadlines surface automatically so nothing is missed
- The dashboard rolls up open, submitted, and approved cases

moduleSales.title: Sales & Agency
moduleSales.body:
A pipeline-focused view for agency-style sales teams, alongside outreach campaigns.

- Pipeline and won value roll up from the same deals as the CRM
- Campaigns reuse the CRM's Campaigns manager — this dashboard just summarizes recent activity
- Use it as a quick daily check before diving into Deals or Campaigns for detail

leads.title: Leads
leads.emptyDescription: Leads arrive automatically from your public website form, or you can add one manually.
leads.newLeadDescription: Manually record an inbound inquiry — the other lead channel besides the public website form.
leads.convertIntro: Promotes this lead into a Contact (and a Company, if a business name is set). The lead itself stays untouched.
leads.suggestEmpty: Get an AI-suggested follow-up article for this lead.
leads.convertedBanner: This lead has already been converted.

contactDetail.aiKnowledgeEmpty: No summary yet. Use the AI assistant below.
contactDetail.nextStepNote: Suggestion: "{suggestion}" — sent to the AI Action Center for approval.
contactDetail.convoSummaryUpdated: Conversation summary updated in the Customer knowledge card.
contactDetail.convoNotFound: No conversation found for this contact.
contactDetail.privacyDescription: Export or erase all personal data held about this contact.

companies.title: Companies
companies.noCompaniesDescription: Add your first company, or create one from lead conversion.
companies.timelineHint: Includes activity from every contact at this company.

activityFeed.title: Activity feed
activityFeed.description: Every meaningful change across the workspace, in one place.
activityFeed.emptyBody: As your team works, important events will appear here.

contracts.title: Contracts
contracts.subjectPlaceholder: Sales process improvement and CRM rollout
contracts.emptyBody: Create your first contract from a contact or deal.
contracts.letterheadDescription: This company letterhead and signatory appear on every contract's header and signature block, and can't be shared until a digital signature has been applied.
contracts.signatureNotConfigured: Set a signatory name under "Letterhead & signature" first.
contracts.emailBodyPrefix: Use the link below to review and accept the contract:

signErrors.locked: This contract is locked and can no longer be signed.
signErrors.no_signatory: Set a signatory name under "Letterhead & signature" before signing.

sendErrors.not_signed: Apply the digital signature before sending.

contracts.printUnsignedNotice: This contract hasn't been digitally signed yet — the signature block won't appear until it is.

publicContract.acceptedByName: This contract has been accepted by {name}.
publicContract.acceptError: Check your name and contract number and try again.

campaigns.title: Email campaigns
campaigns.goalPlaceholder: Invite them back and introduce our new consulting service
campaigns.statsTotal: {count, plural, one {# match in total} other {# matches in total}}
campaigns.statsReachable: {count, plural, one {# reachable by email (this run)} other {# reachable by email (this run)}}
campaigns.emptyBody: Pick a segment and AI will write a personalized email for every recipient.

unfollowedLeads.name: Unfollowed leads (7+ days)
unfollowedLeads.description: Open leads that haven't been followed up on in over a week.

wonCustomers.name: Won customers
wonCustomers.description: Contacts with at least one successful deal.

ops.title: Operations
ops.description: Your tasks, calendar, and files at a glance.
ops.noEvents: Nothing scheduled in the next two weeks.
ops.noTasksBody: Create a task to track your work and deadlines.
ops.noFilesBody: Upload documents to keep them secure and organized.

knowledge.title: Knowledge base
knowledge.description: Short articles the AI assistant draws on to suggest content to leads.

graph.title: Relationships
graph.description: How your companies, contacts, and deals connect.
graph.emptyBody: As you link contacts to companies and deals, the relationship graph fills in.
graph.dragHint: Drag blocks to rearrange · click a block to focus its connections
graph.demoSimulation: Demo mode — connections aren't saved here.
graph.cannotDeleteAuto: This link mirrors real data and can't be removed here.

workflowBuilder.title: Workflow builder
workflowBuilder.description: Design staged processes with required documents, approvals, and SLAs.

aiWorkspace.title: AI
aiWorkspace.description: Your AI assistant proposes; you approve. Nothing changes without your sign-off.
aiWorkspace.providerMockNote: — running in keyless demo mode; add an API key in settings to use a real model.
aiWorkspace.noActivity: No AI activity yet. Try “Summarize” on a contact.
aiWorkspace.noPendingBody: When AI suggests an action, it appears here for your approval before anything changes.

aiAssistant.title: CRM Assistant
aiAssistant.description: Chat with real data — leads, deals, and activity.
aiAssistant.emptyBody: The assistant only answers from what actually exists — it never makes up a number.
aiAssistant.inputPlaceholder: Ask about leads, deals, or activity…
suggestions: How many new leads do we have today?
Which open deals are worth more than €20,000?
How's the sales pipeline looking overall?

admin.settingsDesc: Company profile, branding, and workspace configuration.
admin.auditDesc: A record of security-relevant actions across the workspace.
admin.noAuditBody: Sign-ins, permission changes, exports, and admin actions will appear here.
admin.healthDesc: Live signals from jobs, automation, AI, and integrations.
admin.integrationsDesc: Connect SOBI CRM to other tools via webhooks and the public API.
admin.noWebhooks: No webhooks. Add one to receive events at your endpoint.
admin.copyKeyNotice: This key is shown once. Store it securely.
admin.noApiKeys: No API keys. Create one to access the public API.
admin.modulesDesc: Activate the industry modules your business needs. Each adds workspaces, workflows, and dashboards.
admin.rolesDesc: Roles bundle permissions and are assigned to members. System roles are provided; custom roles can be added.
admin.usersDesc: People with access to this workspace and their roles.
admin.noMembersBody: Invite colleagues to collaborate in this workspace.

studioEntities.noEntitiesBody: Create your own entities with custom fields — they get list views, records, and APIs automatically.
